name: Pell Wants the Source
description: Researcher Pell wants to know who Merchant Prince Cass is buying the black crystals for. If the Ministry restricted this material sixty years ago and Cass has a standing order for it now — someone in the current Ministry knows what it is.
accept_text: She's still looking at the fragment beside her disc when you're ready to go back. 'The question that matters,' she says, 'is not what Cass is doing with it. Cass is a distributor. The question is who commissioned the standing order.' She looks at you. 'Loy works for Cass. Cass has a principal on the other side of this — someone who identified these crystals as valuable eight months ago and reached out through the Merchant Prince network to acquire them without official channels.' She pauses. 'Eight months ago is when the Grid reactivated. Eight months ago is when my instruments at the Core changed. Eight months ago is when the trench crystals started warming and Brec's subjects started shifting resonance and Kira started mapping lattice activity from altitude.' She sets the disc in its case. 'Whoever commissioned Cass's standing order knew what would happen when the Grid reactivated. They were positioned before the event, not after it.' She looks at you. 'That means they knew the Grid was going to reactivate. They had advance information about a sequence that I've been reconstructing from scratch for three years.' She closes the case. 'Ask Loy who Cass received the commission from. He may not know — but he knows what Cass said when she placed the order, and he knows whether she seemed surprised by the commission or whether she was expecting it.' She pauses. 'And ask him how long the standing order has been active. Eight months, or longer.'
complete_text: Loy is quiet when you tell him what Pell found, which is different from his usual quality of ease — this is a different quiet, the quality of someone reassessing what they've been carrying. 'Cass placed the order nine months ago.' He says it. 'One month before the eight-month period you've described.' He looks at the box. 'She told me she had a buyer who was acquiring pre-Cascade material for a private collection. Private collections are a standard category — we don't ask about the collector.' He pauses. 'She did not seem surprised by the commission. She seemed — organized about it. The same way she's organized about things she's been expecting.' He looks at the clouds below. 'I've worked for Cass for eleven years. I know when she's reacting to events and when she's been prepared for them.' He pauses. 'She was prepared for this one.' He is quiet. 'The buyer — I don't have a name. What I have: the delivery specifications. Materials to be delivered to a holding facility in the Under-District of Hive-City, sub-zero level, no manifest entry required.' He meets your eyes. 'Sub-zero levels. I've heard that phrase before in this market. From workers who went down and came back different.' He closes the box. 'I'm going to find out who the buyer is. This is now a professional interest.' He stands. 'Give Pell this information. Tell her: I'm looking for the name. And tell her she should know that whatever this material is — someone knew nine months ago that they were going to want a great deal of it.'
